purpose: Define the Football Truth Intelligence Engine before any further acquisition, provider repair, or canonical promotion work.
problemStatement: The project needs an intelligent football truth layer, not a collection of ad-hoc jobs.
The engine must know competition state, provider capability, missing data, blocked contracts, and allowed next actions.
No search/fetch/promotion runner should be used unless the intelligence engine or registry permits it.

jobGovernance.purpose: Governance board for engine-v1/jobs. This is not an execution plan.
rules: Do not create new jobs until the governance board is reviewed.
Do not run QUARANTINE_DO_NOT_RUN_DIRECTLY jobs manually.
Do not use schema-specific promotion builders outside their exact input contract.
Provider normalizers must be registered in Provider Contract Registry before reuse.
Diagnostic-only jobs may inform decisions but must not drive production writes.
Canonical writes require a dry-run report with zero blocked rows and explicit production flags.

providerRegistry.purpose: Seed Provider Contract Registry for Football Truth intelligence engine. This is a governance/design manifest, not an execution job.
rules: No provider acquisition/search/fetch runner may run unless a provider contract row allows it.
No promotion adapter may be used outside its exact schema contract.
Provider normalizers are reusable only when registered here with capability, source job, and promotion path.
Blocked providers must remain blocked until a contract repair proves normalized rows.
Canonical writes require known provider contract, dry-run, zero blocked rows, zero plan errors, and explicit production flags.

providerContracts.notes: Sweden fixtures/results and standings already promoted. Keep as proven contract reference.
Do not promote SPFL fixtures until safe official id-to-team-name map exists.
IRL2 is explicitly partial results-only; no complete fixture calendar claim.
Finland standings remain derived-only unless official standings are found/accepted.
Belgian cup winner/final canonical state written.

engineResponsibilities.name: Competition State Resolver
engineResponsibilities.goal: Classify each competition as active, completed, upcoming, off-season, blocked, or unknown.
mustNotUse: standings alone as proof of active season
partial fixture rows as full calendar proof

engineResponsibilities.name: Provider Capability Resolver
engineResponsibilities.goal: Know which providers can supply fixtures, results, standings, cup winners, and season state.
mustNotUse: generic HTML parsing without provider contract
schema-specific promotion adapters across unrelated providers

engineResponsibilities.name: Missing Data Board
engineResponsibilities.goal: For every competition, determine what is missing and whether the missing piece is actionable, blocked, or unknown.

engineResponsibilities.name: Next Batch Action Planner
engineResponsibilities.goal: Create large batch actions by provider/contract, not league-by-league.

engineResponsibilities.name: Promotion Readiness Gate
engineResponsibilities.goal: Allow canonical writes only after verified normalized rows and dry-run success.

allowedOnlyAfter: no fetch/search/canonical write policy is embedded
